decisions.title: Prioritize Canvas documentation completion before feature freeze
decisions.context: DOC-33 Canvas docs in flight, critical for Embed Deeper pillar. Canvas drives decision capture by enabling analysts to build sharable workflows. Q2 product launches require supporting docs.

arguments.for: Canvas is key differentiator for Embed Deeper strategy. Without docs, adoption stalls and decision capture metrics won't move. Getting Started (DOC-21) shows doc velocity is good.
arguments.against: Engineering could ship faster without waiting for docs. Could launch beta without full documentation. Risk: low adoption without proper onboarding materials.

decisions.title: Formalize Files in CEL as core platform capability vs. experimental feature
decisions.context: ENG-8700 RFC, ENG-10014 instrumentation, ENG-10015 UI attachment, ENG-10013 YAML support. Files enable asset-based workflows central to Signal>Evidence>Decision. Significant engineering investment already committed.

arguments.for: Clear product trajectory with RFC + 4 implementation tickets. Files unlock new decision workflows. YAML support suggests production intent. Aligns with Nail the Core (CEL platform stability).
arguments.against: Still experimental - could be feature creep. Offline file backing (ENG-6764) not yet solved. May distract from core Canvas/Explore V2 priorities. Need usage data before committing.

decisions.title: Accelerate Sift Edge production readiness for Q2 enterprise deployments
decisions.context: ENG-10137 metrics docs improvement, ENG-9820 back pressure metrics. Sift Edge is Build the Moat pillar - competitive differentiator for edge deployment. Metrics work suggests productization phase.
decisions.blockedBy: Incomplete observability stack - back pressure metrics added but docs lag. No clear SLA definitions for enterprise customers. GovCloud deployment (ENG-9939) may require Edge support.

arguments.for: Edge is unique moat capability. Early enterprise interest. Metrics instrumentation shows engineering maturity. GovCloud customer needs offline/edge capabilities.
arguments.against: Metrics docs improvements suggest current state isn't enterprise-ready. Could drain resources from Canvas/Explore V2. Market demand unclear vs. investment required.

decisions.title: Resolve family candidate/archived runs handling before Explore V2 launch
decisions.context: ENG-10139 stop gap, ENG-9924 archived handling, ENG-7853 minimum input runs. Family stats critical for Explore V2 comparative analysis. Stop-gap solutions suggest underlying architecture issue.

arguments.for: Stop-gap ticket signals urgent prod issue. Family candidates enable multi-run comparisons = core decision workflow. Architectural fix needed before Explore V2 GA or tech debt compounds.
arguments.against: Stop-gap may be sufficient for launch. Perfect solution could delay Explore V2. Users may not hit archived run edge cases frequently. Fast follow acceptable.

decisions.title: Commit to Python Rules GA timeline or deprioritize for 2026
decisions.context: No Python Rules tickets in current sprint signals. Product identified as 2026 key product but no visible engineering velocity. Rules enable programmatic decision automation - high strategy value.

arguments.for: Lack of tickets suggests drift from strategy. Need explicit go/no-go to reallocate resources. Python Rules could be high-leverage for Embed Deeper (API-first workflows).
arguments.against: May be in earlier research phase not reflected in Linear. Other products (Canvas, Files) showing strong momentum. Could be right to defer if market signal weak.

decisions.title: Standardize feature flag management before technical debt blocks velocity
decisions.context: ENG-8723 database-backed feature flag tooling, ENG-10058 removing channel search replica flag. Feature flags proliferating across platform - need management discipline for Nail the Core.

arguments.for: Feature flag removal ticket suggests cleanup needed. Database-backed tooling indicates professionalization. Unmanaged flags create technical debt that slows all product development.
arguments.against: Not customer-facing - opportunity cost vs. Canvas/Explore work. Current flag system may be working adequately. Could be premature optimization.

timeAudit.weekSummary: High engineering execution week - 30+ tickets active across all pillars. Risk: diffusion across too many initiatives. Canvas docs, Files in CEL, Explore V2, Edge metrics, GovCloud all moving simultaneously. Need decision on Python Rules absence.
alerts: No Python Rules engineering activity despite being named 2026 key product - requires strategic decision by Friday
Stop-gap solutions appearing (ENG-10139) suggests architectural issues need dedicated focus time
Ask SIFT product has zero signals in current sprint - validate if still 2026 priority
Feature flag technical debt accumulating - ENG-8723 tooling needs prioritization decision
North Star metric (Decisions Captured) not instrumented in any tickets - cannot measure progress
